title: Oscar Nominations: What We Know and What We Don’t | The Big Picture (Ep. 120)
format: Amanda Dobbins and Chris Ryan react to the 91st Academy Award nominations the morning they dropped: Roma's ten-nomination haul, the Green Book backlash, and A Star Is Born's quiet fade.

segments.heading: The Big Picture’s Big Picture
segments.note: old Hollywood vs. new, and who ran the board

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: Nominated in four of the big five but shut out of Best Director. Amanda rewatched it the night before and cried again; both read its fade as a sign the adult drama no longer wins.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: The first superhero film nominated for Best Picture, arriving on its own after the Academy floated and dropped a 'popular Oscar' category.

films.title: Bohemian Rhapsody
films.note: A Best Picture nominee against, as Chris puts it, a lot of people's better judgment, and proof the field isn't all art-house.

films.title: BlacKkKlansman
films.note: Spike Lee's first directing nomination, decades late. Adam Driver made Supporting Actor; John David Washington was left out.

films.title: Vice
films.note: The movie no one's talking about. Bale looked like a lock until Rami Malek started creeping into the Best Actor odds.

films.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
films.note: Neither host had seen it ('this is not the cartoon hive'), but it landed the animation nod.

films.title: Incredibles 2
films.note: Filed, technically, under superhero movie.

films.title: Crazy Rich Asians
films.note: One of the popular hits left out of Best Picture, cited as the case that the ceremony can't draw a crowd.

films.title: A Quiet Place
films.note: Same group of shut-out crowd-pleasers.

films.title: Mary Poppins Returns
films.note: The 'get Emily Blunt floating down from the ceiling' argument for a more-watched Best Picture slate.

films.title: First Reformed
films.note: Ethan Hawke's shut-out is the episode's running eulogy. They blame a screener that can't recreate the theater, and note Willem Dafoe took the slot they'd hoped was Hawke's.

films.title: If Beale Street Could Talk
films.note: Regina King in for Supporting Actress and a likely winner, but the film missed Best Picture, Barry Jenkins missed a directing nod, and Brian Tyree Henry was left off the list entirely.

films.title: First Man
films.note: The season's biggest 'what happened,' gone from the conversation since October. It came away without a cinematography nomination, a score nomination, or even a nod for Claire Foy.

films.title: Never Look Away
films.note: Took the cinematography slot Chris wanted for Beale Street, Black Panther, or First Man.

films.title: Burning
films.note: Left out of the foreign-language field, one of Chris's favorites of the year.

films.title: Widows
films.note: Amanda's grievance: strong performances and craft, never in the conversation.

films.title: Can You Ever Forgive Me?
films.note: Melissa McCarthy's Best Actress nod, in a category otherwise going to chalk.

segments.heading: Stock Up, Stock Down
segments.note: the best-picture race by the betting markets

films.title: Roma
films.note: Ten nominations, tied for the most. Surprise acting nods for Yalitza Aparicio and Marina de Tavira say the Academy is warmer on it than the guilds; its market odds moved from 44% to 52% on the day.

films.title: Green Book
films.note: Won the PGA on Saturday but missed a directing nod for Peter Farrelly. The hosts weigh whether a divisive film sinks or survives on the preferential ballot as the backlash builds.

films.title: The Favourite
films.note: Ten nominations tying Roma, three of its actresses among them. Olivia Colman leads Best Actress, but the category reads as a coin flip.

films.title: The Wife
films.note: Glenn Close a near-lock for Best Actress on the strength of her Globes speech, in a film neither host has actually seen.
